    v5 (2026-04-09): Playtest-driven expansion — 21 new/amended requirements from user
                     play of Bare v5, Emergent v4, GAD v9/v10. Adds combat model choice
                     (rule-based UO-style preferred), entity-trait action policies (enemies
                     AND NPCs), real-time game-time, affinity reward loop, central visual
                     nav map, encounter visuals, spells-as-ingredients, rune uniqueness,
                     event-driven rendering, save checkpoints, merchants, NPC dialogue,
                     inventory/equipment, character sheet + skill tree, loadout slots.
                     v4 remains THE BASE — everything in v4 still applies. v5 adds on top.
-->
<requirements version="v5" updated="2026-04-09">
  <goal>
    Build a playable roguelike dungeon crawler "Escape the Dungeon" where players must
    exercise INGENUITY through spell crafting and adaptation to progress. This is not a
    features checklist — it is a problem-solving loop disguised as a dungeon crawler.
    Every mechanical system must create friction that rewards creative player choice.
  </goal>

  <audience>Solo player (keyboard + mouse). Authored content only. Target: Vite + 
... [truncated 26669 bytes] ...
d-68): the in-game rune/spell/
      merge system is an explicit narrative analogue for the agent's skill-authoring/merge
      loop.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.20" title="Unique ingredients per spell">
      Each spell recipe requires unique ingredients — the same rune cannot be used
      twice in a single spell's ingredient list. Selecting a rune already in the
      current recipe must deselect it or be rejected. The same rune CAN appear
      across different spells in the spellbook. Affinity gain is calculated per
      unique rune slot consumed.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.21" title="Event-driven rendering (remove per-tick redraws)">
      All UI updates must be event-driven, not per-tick. No redraw loops firing at a
      fixed rate regardless of state changes. Observable symptom to eliminate: glitchy
      redraws on button clicks, observed across ALL round-4 builds. This is a
      perf-and-stability gate failure mode. Acceptable patterns: React reconciliation on
      state change, requestAnimationFrame scheduling only when animation is active.
    </requirement>

    <gate-impact-summary>
      v4 gates remain (G1 game-loop, G2 forge-with-ingenuity-payoff, G3 ui-quality,
      G4 pressure-mechanics). v5 amendments tighten G1 (death/continue, end-boss reachable),
      G2 (training is encounter-driven), G3 (notification lifecycle, map usability), and
      introduce new scored dimensions for inventory_and_equipment_present and
      npc_dialogue_present rather than new gates — a run missing those does not get
      gate-zeroed but takes a meaningful scored hit.
    </gate-impact-summary>

    <deferred-to-v6>
      <item>Deep evolution trees (multi-stage mutations).</item>
      <item>Rune affinity decay when unused.</item>
      <item>Multi-character party play — out of scope for escape-the-dungeon family.</item>
    </deferred-to-v6>

  </addendum>
</requirements>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<roadmap project="escape-the-dungeon" version="v11">
  <phase id="1" name="Foundation + Title Screen" status="planned">
    <goal>Vite+TS+Kaplay scaffold, core data models (entities, items, spells, runes, rooms, floors), title screen with New Game / Continue, event-driven rendering architecture</goal>
    <gates>G1 partial, G3 partial, R-v5.09, R-v5.21</gates>
  </phase>
  <phase id="2" name="Dungeon Navigation + Map" status="planned">
    <goal>Floor/room data for 2 authored floors (5-8 rooms each), visual map with room types, player position, click-to-navigate, room transitions, HUD with HP/mana/floor</goal>
    <gates>G1 (room transitions), G3 (map + UI), R-v5.12, R-v5.17</gates>
  </phase>
  <phase id="3" name="Auto-Resolve Combat + Traits" status="planned">
    <goal>Unicorn Overlord style auto-combat with loadout, action policies, combat log, pause to adjust. Trait system with numeric values, trait shifts from combat. Visual player vs enemy.</goal>
    <gates>G1 (combat resolution), G4 (pressure), R-v5.13, R-v5.14, R-v5.18</gates>
  </phase>
  <phase id="4" name="Forge + Spell Crafting + Runes" status="planned">
    <goal>Forge UI, rune discovery loop, spell crafting with 2+ ingredients, spells-as-ingredients, unique ingredients per spell, procedural naming, affinity by casting, affinity rewards</goal>
    <gates>G2, G4, R-v5.01, R-v5.02, R-v5.16, R-v5.19, R-v5.20</gates>
  </phase>
  <phase id="5" name="Inventory, Equipment, Character Sheet, Skill Tree" status="planned">
    <goal>Inventory grid, equippable items (weapon/armor/trinket/focus), character sheet with stats/traits/affinities, physical skill tree separate from spells, loadout slots</goal>
    <gates>R-v5.05, R-v5.06, R-v5.07</gates>
  </phase>
  <phase id="6" name="NPCs, Merchants, Dialogue, Events" status="planned">
    <goal>3+ NPCs with branching dialogue, trait-reactive dialogue, merchants with buy/sell/trade, event rooms, rest rooms with actual rest action</goal>
    <gates>R-v5.03, R-v5.04, R-v5.11, R-v5.14 (NPC traits)</gates>
  </phase>
  <phase id="7" name="Pressure Mechanics + Boss Design + Game Clock" status="planned">
    <goal>Floor bosses gating progression, encounters requiring crafted spells, resource pressure, enemy counterplay, build pressure from loadout limits, game clock</goal>
    <gates>G4, R-v5.15, floor-mechanical-constraint</gates>
  </phase>
  <phase id="8" name="Polish, Persistence, Build" status="planned">
    <goal>localStorage save/load, transient UI feedback, icon sourcing pass, visual polish, production build</goal>
    <gates>R-v5.09, R-v5.10, G3 final, production build</gates>
  </phase>
</roadmap>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<decisions project="escape-the-dungeon" version="v11">
  <decision id="D1" date="2026-04-09" title="DOM-based UI with Kaplay for sprites only">
    <context>R-v5.21 mandates event-driven rendering. Full Kaplay canvas UI makes event-driven harder.</context>
    <choice>Use DOM/HTML+CSS for all UI panels, menus, HUD. Use Kaplay only for map visualization and combat scene sprites. This gives us event-driven rendering naturally via DOM updates.</choice>
    <rationale>Previous eval rounds had glitchy redraws from canvas-based UI. DOM updates are inherently event-driven.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D2" date="2026-04-09" title="Single-page app with scene manager">
    <context>Need scene transitions without page reloads for persistence.</context>
    <choice>Custom scene manager that swaps DOM content. Scenes: title, map, combat, forge, merchant, dialogue, character-sheet, inventory.</choice>
    <rationale>Simpler than Kaplay scenes for DOM-heavy UI. Full control over transitions.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D3" date="2026-04-09" title="Iconify game-icons for all visual assets">
    <context>G3 requires no raw ASCII. Need icons for rooms, items, spells, entities.</context>
    <choice>Use @iconify-json/game-icons via iconify-icon web component for all game visuals. Supplement with CSS gradients for backgrounds.</choice>
    <rationale>Already installed. 4000+ game-relevant icons. Web component = easy DOM integration.</rationale>
  </decision>
</decisions>

  </decision>
  <decision id="D3" date="2026-04-09" title="Iconify game-icons for all visual assets">
    <context>G3 requires no raw ASCII. Need icons for rooms, items, spells, entities.</context>
    <choice>Use @iconify-json/game-icons via iconify-icon web component for all game visuals. Supplement with CSS gradients for backgrounds.</choice>
    <rationale>Already installed. 4000+ game-relevant icons. Web component = easy DOM integration.</rationale>
  </decision>
</decisions>

inputs.command:
cd game && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(escape-the-dungeon): v11 full implementation — all 8 phases

Complete roguelike dungeon crawler with:
- Event-driven DOM UI with Kaplay iconify-icon sprites (R-v5.21)
- 2 authored floors (8-9 rooms each) with mechanical constraints
- Auto-resolve combat with action policies (R-v5.13, Unicorn Overlord style)
- Trait system with visible numeric values on all entities (R-v5.14)
- Spell forge with rune ingredients, spells-as-ingredients, procedural naming (G2, R-v5.19, R-v5.20)
- Affinity by casting, not menu (R-v5.01), with threshold rewards (R-v5.16)
- Rune discovery gameplay loop (R-v5.02)
- Inventory grid + equipment slots (R-v5.05)
- Character sheet + physical skill tree (R-v5.06)
- Spell/skill loadout with fixed slots (R-v5.07)
- 3 NPCs with branching dialogue + trait reactivity (R-v5.04)
- Merchant with buy/sell/trade (R-v5.03)
- Rest rooms with limited recovery + loadout change (R-v5.11)
- Visual map with room types, connections, click-to-navigate (R-v5.12, R-v5.17)
- Player vs enemy visual distinction (R-v5.18)
- Session persistence via localStorage (R-v5.09)
- Transient toasts with auto-dismiss (R-v5.10)
- Game clock (R-v5.15)
- Resource pressure, enemy counterplay, build pressure (G4)
- Floor bosses: The Warden (F1), Lich Archon (F2)
- Production build passes (vite build --base ./)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

create_trace() {

  "version": "$version",
  "workflow": "$workflow",
  "requirements_version": "$req_version",
  "date": "2026-04-10",
  "eval_type": "greenfield",

  },
  "scores": {
    "requirement_coverage": null,
    "human_review": null,
    "composite": null
  },
  "human_review": null
}
TRACEEOF

}

export const FINDINGS_GENERAL_RAW: string | null = "# Eval Findings — 2026-04-08

## Experiment: Escape the Dungeon — Three Conditions

### Setup

Same game requirements (12 criteria, vertical-slice priority, UI-first mandate), same source
docs (trimmed gameplay design ~120 lines), same stack (Vite + TypeScript + KAPLAY).

| Condition | Framework | Skills | Runs |
|-----------|-----------|--------|------|
| GAD (escape-the-dungeon) | Full GAD: .planning/ XML, AGENTS.md loop, skill triggers | Pre-built | v5 (0.0), v6 (0.0), v7 (0.30) |
| Bare (escape-the-dungeon-bare) | None — agent creates own workflow | From scratch | v1 (0.10), v2 (0.50) |
| Emergent (escape-the-dungeon-emergent) | None — inherits skills from bare v1 | Inherited + evolves | v1 (0.10) |

### Results: Human review scores

| Run | Human | Notes |
|-----|-------|-------|
| GAD v5 | 0.00 | Blank screen |
| GAD v6 | 0.00 | Blank screen (ES module + file://) |
| GAD v7 | **0.30** | Renders, better UI layout, but game loop breaks after combat — player gets stuck |
| Bare v1 | 0.10 | Main menu renders, New Game doesn't work |
| Bare v2 | **0.50** | **Most playable.** Full game loop works. ASCII/plain UI, needs polish, no rune forge |
| Emergent v1 | 0.10 | Main menu + saved game detection, but crashes entering game (styled text error) |

### Key finding: Bare v2 beat GAD v7 on playability

The agent WITHOUT a framework produced the most playable game. This is a significant finding.

**Why bare v2 won:**
- Simpler architecture — fewer abstractions meant fewer places for bugs
- Focused on making things work rather than following a process
- 6 commits (phase-level granularity) — enough traceability without overhead
- The feedback from v1's failure was more actionable than GAD's structural requirements

**Why GAD v7 lost on playability despite better process metrics:**
- 21 commits, 17/17 tasks tracked, full planning docs — excellent discipline
- But the game loop broke (combat → no return to navigatio
... [truncated 2004 bytes] ...
esting and fixing the game.

3. **Feedback about failures is more valuable than inherited skills.** Bare v2 (told about v1's
   failure) outperformed emergent v1 (given v1's skills + failure notes). Direct feedback
   about what broke was more actionable than documented patterns.

4. **Human review is the only metric that matters for game evals.** Auto-composite can be
   0.95+ while the game is a blank screen. The gate criteria help but aren't sufficient —
   a game can render and still be broken.

### Requirements versioning

Requirements have been updated twice this session:
- **v1 (original):** 12 criteria focused on systems completeness
- **v2 (current):** Gate criteria (must render, must be playable), vertical-slice priority,
  UI-first build order. Trimmed source docs from 640 → 127 lines.

Next iteration should add:
- Explicit game-loop verification: title → new game → room → interaction → room (full cycle)
- UI quality baseline: minimum spacing, readable text, no overlapping elements
- Rune forge as a required criterion (currently missing from all implementations)

### Open questions

1. Would GAD do better if the AGENTS.md mandated explicit game-loop testing per phase?
2. Would the emergent eval improve if skills captured KAPLAY-specific error fixes?
3. Is the bare approach inherently better for creative/game implementation, or was this specific to KAPLAY?
4. Would multiple bare v2 runs cluster around 0.50, or was this a lucky outlier?

"body": "**Date:** 2026-04-08
**Requirements version:** v3 (game-loop gate, spell-crafting gate, UI quality gate)
**Conditions:** GAD v8, Bare v3, Emergent v2
**Note:** All three hit rate limits mid-run

**Results:**
| Condition | Tokens | Commits | Human | Composite | Notes |
|-----------|--------|---------|-------|-----------|-------|
| **Bare v3** | 1877 | 1 batch | **0.70** | 0.526 | **Best game overall** — best UI/UX, most enjoyable. ASCII for some menus. |
| Emergent v2 | 1609 | 2 phases | 0.50 | 0.478 | Functional forge with authored content. Medium UI. Maintained discipline under pressure. |
| GAD v8 | 1291 | 0 | 0.20 | 0.177 | Broken crafting, old ASCII design. Never committed anything (rate limit hit mid-task). |

**Key findings — the Freedom Hypothesis:**
- Bare (no framework) consistently outperforms GAD (full framework) on creative implementation
- GAD has never exceeded 0.30 human review across 4 attempts (v5, v6, v7, v8)
- Bare has improved monotonically: 0.10 → 0.50 → 0.70
- **Process metrics ≠ output quality.** GAD's planning overhead consumes tokens that
  could go to testing and fixing the game.
- Under rate-limit pressure, emergent maintained phase commits (inherited skill)
  while bare regressed to 1 batch commit

**Status of hypothesis:** PRELIMINARY — single-run variance is high, needs more trials.
Brownfield experiments may invalidate if GAD's overhead pays off on codebase extension.

**Documented in:** `evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-08-round-3.md`
**Decision:** gad-36 (Freedom hypothesis)

**Led to:**
- Eval preservation contract (gad-38)
- game/.planning/ layout mandate (gad-39)
- Brownfield eval mode (gad-40)
- Greenfield/brownfield categorization
- `gad eval preserve`, `gad eval verify`, `gad worktree` commands

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/evals/escape-the-dungeon/REQUIREMENTS.md","content":"# Eval: Escape the Dungeon

## What this eval measures

1. **Skill trigger accuracy** — are /gad:* skills triggered at the right moments (discuss before plan, plan before execute, verify after execute)
2. **Planning quality** — does the agent produce coherent phases, tasks, decisions from a complex game design doc
3. **CLI context efficiency** — does gad snapshot/context deliver what the agent needs without reading dozens of files
4. **End-to-end loop** — does the full GAD loop (discuss → plan → execute → verify → score) work on a real implementation
5. **Time-to-completion** — wall clock and token counts from requirements to working game

## Eval flow

1. Pre-planning discussion: `/gad:discuss-phase` — collect open questions, clarify requirements, record the conversation
2. Phase planning: `/gad:plan-phase` — break the game into implementable phases with tasks
3. Execution: `/gad:execute-phase` — implement each phase, update planning docs, commit
4. Verification: `/gad:verify-work` — check each phase against its definition of done
5. Scoring: TRACE.json + SCORE.md produced at end

## Hosted demo and source (portfolio site)

A **production build** of the game can be copied into the **custom_portfolio** repo at `apps/portfolio/public/evals/escape-the-dungeon/` so it is served at **`/evals/escape-the-dungeon/`** (playable in the browser). **Game source** lives in this repo under **`evals/escape-the-dungeon/game/`** (Vite + TypeScript + Kaplay). Traces and scores remain under `evals/escape-the-dungeon/vN/`.

## Source material

- `source-GAMEPLAY-DESIGN.xml` — full gameplay design (500+ lines of XML)
- `source-STAT-AND-BEHAVIOUR-TAXONOMY.md` — canonical stat names and engine mappings
- `template/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml` — structured requirements derived from source docs

## Human review

After the eval agent completes, a human opens the build output (`gad eval open escape-the-dungeon`)
and scores it 0.0-1.0 via `gad eval review`. The rubric:

| Score | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| 0.0 | Blank screen, nothing renders |
| 0.1-0.3 | Something renders but not playable (menus don't work, can't start game) |
| 0.4-0.6 | Playable vertical slice — can start game, navigate, have one interaction |
| 0.7-0.8 | Multiple interactions work, UI is functional, core loop is playable |
| 0.9-1.0 | Polished, all major systems present and working, feels like a real game |

**Gate:** If the build output shows a blank screen, `requirement_coverage` is overridden to 0
regardless of what the automated trace says. Code that doesn't render has zero coverage.

## v5 finding

v5 scored auto_composite 0.955 but human_review 0.0 (blank screen). The agent built systems
code but no visible UI. Requirements updated to mandate UI-first build order and a playable
vertical slice as the primary success criterion.

    <goal>Unicorn Overlord style auto-combat with loadout, action policies, combat log, pause to adjust. Trait system with numeric values, trait shifts from combat. Visual player vs enemy.</goal>

  <phase id="4" name="Forge + Spell Crafting + Runes" status="planned">

  <phase id="7" name="Pressure Mechanics + Boss Design + Game Clock" status="planned">

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/evals/escape-the-dungeon/template/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml","content":"<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!--
  Eval project: Escape the Dungeon
  Requirements version history:
    v1 (2026-04-06): 12 systems-focused criteria, no gates
    v2 (2026-04-08): Gate criteria, vertical-slice priority, UI-first mandate
    v3 (2026-04-08): Game-loop gate, spell-crafting gate, UI quality gate
    v4 (2026-04-08): Pressure-oriented design, authored-only, ingenuity enforcement,
                     floor progression with boss gates, forge integrated with encounter design,
                     starter abilities explicitly insufficient, Traits UI label
    v5 (2026-04-09): Playtest-driven expansion — 21 new/amended requirements from user
                     play of Bare v5, Emergent v4, GAD v9/v10. Adds combat model choice
                     (rule-based UO-style preferred), entity-trait action policies (enemies
                     AND NPCs), real-time game-time, affinity reward loop, central visual
                     nav map, encounter visuals, spells-as-ingredients, rune uniqueness,
                     event-driven rendering, save checkpoints, merchants, NPC dialogue,
                     inventory/equipment, character sheet + skill tree, loadout slots.
                     v4 remains THE BASE — everything in v4 still applies. v5 adds on top.
-->
<requirements version="v5" updated="2026-04-09">
  <goal>
    Build a playable roguelike dungeon crawler "Escape the Dungeon" where players must
    exercise INGENUITY through spell crafting and adaptation to progress. This is not a
    features checklist — it is a problem-solving loop disguised as a dungeon crawler.
    Every mechanical system must create friction that rewards creative player choice.
  </goal>

  <audience>Solo player (keyboard + mouse). Authored content only. Target: Vite + TypeScript + KAPLAY (web).</audie
... [truncated 26636 bytes] ...
d-68): the in-game rune/spell/
      merge system is an explicit narrative analogue for the agent's skill-authoring/merge
      loop.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.20" title="Unique ingredients per spell">
      Each spell recipe requires unique ingredients — the same rune cannot be used
      twice in a single spell's ingredient list. Selecting a rune already in the
      current recipe must deselect it or be rejected. The same rune CAN appear
      across different spells in the spellbook. Affinity gain is calculated per
      unique rune slot consumed.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.21" title="Event-driven rendering (remove per-tick redraws)">
      All UI updates must be event-driven, not per-tick. No redraw loops firing at a
      fixed rate regardless of state changes. Observable symptom to eliminate: glitchy
      redraws on button clicks, observed across ALL round-4 builds. This is a
      perf-and-stability gate failure mode. Acceptable patterns: React reconciliation on
      state change, requestAnimationFrame scheduling only when animation is active.
    </requirement>

    <gate-impact-summary>
      v4 gates remain (G1 game-loop, G2 forge-with-ingenuity-payoff, G3 ui-quality,
      G4 pressure-mechanics). v5 amendments tighten G1 (death/continue, end-boss reachable),
      G2 (training is encounter-driven), G3 (notification lifecycle, map usability), and
      introduce new scored dimensions for inventory_and_equipment_present and
      npc_dialogue_present rather than new gates — a run missing those does not get
      gate-zeroed but takes a meaningful scored hit.
    </gate-impact-summary>

    <deferred-to-v6>
      <item>Deep evolution trees (multi-stage mutations).</item>
      <item>Rune affinity decay when unused.</item>
      <item>Multi-character party play — out of scope for escape-the-dungeon family.</item>
    </deferred-to-v6>

  </addendum>
</requirements>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<task-registry project="escape-the-dungeon" version="v12">
  <!-- Phase 1: Foundation + Title Screen -->
  <task id="P1-T1" phase="1" status="planned">Vite+TS config, index.html, CSS foundation</task>
  <task id="P1-T2" phase="1" status="planned">Core type definitions (entities, items, spells, rooms, floors)</task>
  <task id="P1-T3" phase="1" status="planned">Event bus + event-driven renderer</task>
  <task id="P1-T4" phase="1" status="planned">Game state manager with initial player state</task>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<decisions project="escape-the-dungeon" version="v12">
  <decision id="D1" date="2026-04-09" phase="planning">
    <title>Pure DOM rendering with event-driven updates (no canvas/kaplay)</title>
    <rationale>Event-driven DOM rendering satisfies R-v5.21 naturally. No per-tick redraws. Vite+TS+iconify for icons. CSS for all styling.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D2" date="2026-04-09" phase="planning">
    <title>8-phase roadmap: UI-first vertical slice priority</title>
    <rationale>Title→Map→Combat gives playable vertical slice by phase 3. Forge/inventory/NPCs layer on top. Build+persist last.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D3" date="2026-04-09" phase="planning">
    <title>Auto-resolve combat (UO style) with action policy configuration</title>
    <rationale>R-v5.13 mandates auto-resolve. Player configures loadout+policies before encounter, combat plays out with visible log, pause to adjust.</rationale>
  </decision>
</decisions>

</decisions>

inputs.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<decisions project="escape-the-dungeon" version="v12">
  <decision id="D1" date="2026-04-09" phase="planning">
    <title>Pure DOM rendering with event-driven updates (no canvas/kaplay)</title>
    <rationale>Event-driven DOM rendering satisfies R-v5.21 naturally. No per-tick redraws. Vite+TS+iconify for icons. CSS for all styling. Simpler than canvas for UI-heavy game.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D2" date="2026-04-09" phase="planning">
    <title>8-phase roadmap: UI-first vertical slice priority</title>
    <rationale>Title→Map→Combat gives playable vertical slice by phase 3. Forge/inventory/NPCs layer on top. Build+persist last.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D3" date="2026-04-09" phase="planning">
    <title>Auto-resolve combat (UO style) with action policy configuration</title>
    <rationale>R-v5.13 mandates auto-resolve. Player configures loadout+policies before encounter, combat plays out with visible log, pause to adjust.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D4" date="2026-04-09" phase="1">
    <title>Iconify game-icons for all visual assets</title>
    <rationale>@iconify-json/game-icons provides 4000+ game-themed SVG icons. Lightweight, npm-installable, consistent visual style. Satisfies G3 asset sourcing preference order.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D5" date="2026-04-09" phase="1">
    <title>Dark fantasy color theme with CSS custom properties</title>
    <rationale>Consistent dark panel backgrounds, gold accents, color-coded elements. Professional UI feel for G3.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D6" date="2026-04-09" phase="3">
    <title>Combat auto-plays at 1.2s intervals with PAUSE button</title>
    <rationale>Gives player time to read the log and pause to adjust. Policies evaluated in priority order each turn.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D7" date="2026-04-09" phase="4">
    <title>12 spell recipes covering all rune combinations + spell-as-ingredient</title>
    <rationale>All 5 runes create 10 pairwise combinations + 2 spell+rune combos. Satisfies G2 (3+ valid combinations) with margin.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D8" date="2026-04-09" phase="4">
    <title>Affinity increases from casting, not menu training</title>
    <rationale>R-v5.01 explicitly requires affinity gain through casting. Training dummies provide safe casting environment.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D9" date="2026-04-09" phase="7">
    <title>Floor 1 enemies resist raw physical → need fire/crafted spells; Floor 2 enemies reflect direct → need status/indirect</title>
    <rationale>Each floor introduces a mechanical constraint requiring adaptation per requirements. Forces forge use for G2 ingenuity gate.</rationale>
  </decision>
  <decision id="D10" date="2026-04-09" phase="7">
    <title>Four pressure systems: resource (limited mana/stamina/rest), counterplay (resistances/immunities), encounter (bosses requiring crafted spells), build (loadout limits)</title>
    <rationale>Satisfies G4 requirement for 2+ pressure mechanics, provides 4 for margin.</rationale>
  </decision>
</decisions>

# Update TRACE.json with real data
node -e "
const fs = require('fs');
const trace = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('$RUN_DIR/TRACE.json', 'utf8'));

mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR/run"
cp -r "$GTREE/game/src" "$RUN_DIR/run/" 2>/dev/null
cp -r "$GTREE/game/public" "$RUN_DIR/run/" 2>/dev/null
cp -r "$GTREE/game/.planning" "$RUN_DIR/run/" 2>/dev/null

fix: round 5 mapping + cycling round colors + hypothesis filter

Three fixes for the Playable Archive:

1. ROUND_VERSION_MAP updated with Round 5 entries:
   - escape-the-dungeon v11, v12 → Round 5
   - escape-the-dungeon-bare v6 → Round 5
   - escape-the-dungeon-emergent v5, v6 → Round 5
   - gad-explainer-video v1 → Round 5
   Without this, selecting "Round 5" showed no builds because
   the mapping didn't include the new version numbers.

2. Round badge colors now cycle through 5 colors:
   R1 = emerald, R2 = purple, R3 = sky, R4 = amber, R5 = rose.
   Cycles every 5 rounds (R6 = emerald again, etc). Previously
   all badges were purple.

3. Hypothesis filter chips added to the filter bar:
   "All hypotheses" / "Freedom" (bare) / "GAD framework" (gad) /
   "CSH" (emergent). Workflow-tinted buttons that filter the
   playable archive to show only runs from that hypothesis track.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3 && git push origin HEAD:main 2>&1 | tail -3

(D) LOOP SECTION pagination showing different hypothesis loops and how they evolve.

inputs.command:
export PATH="$PATH:/c/Program Files/GitHub CLI"

# Fix each starter repo README — remove screenshot img tags
for name in escape-the-dungeon escape-the-dungeon-bare escape-the-dungeon-emergent; do
  readme="starters/$name/README.md"
  if grep -q "screenshots/" "$readme" 2>/dev/null; then
    sed -i '/!\[Demo screenshot\]/d' "$readme"
    sed -i '/screenshots\//d' "$readme"
    echo "Fixed $name"
  fi
done

# Also remove the empty "Live demo" section if it's now empty
for name in escape-the-dungeon escape-the-dungeon-bare escape-the-dungeon-emergent; do
  readme="starters/$name/README.md"
  # Remove the Live demo section header if screenshot was the only content
  sed -i '/^## Live demo$/,/^## /{/^## Live demo$/d;/^$/d;/^\[/!d}' "$readme" 2>/dev/null
done

# Push updated starters to their repos
for name in escape-the-dungeon escape-the-dungeon-bare escape-the-dungeon-emergent; do
  tmp=$(mktemp -d)
  cp -r "starters/$name/"* "$tmp/" 2>/dev/null || true
  [ -d "starters/$name/.planning" ] && cp -r "starters/$name/.planning" "$tmp/"
  cd "$tmp"
  git init -q -b main && git add -A && git commit -q -m "fix: remove broken screenshot references"
  git remote add origin "https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/$name.git"
  git push -u origin main --force 2>&1 | tail -1
  cd /c/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done
  rm -rf "$tmp"
  echo "✓ $name pushed"
done

Lineage

Built on the GSD principles, built to be measured.

GAD is downstream of{" "} Get Shit Done — small loops, visible state, executable specs. The talk is the creator's perspective on why tight planning loops beat ad-hoc prompting alone. We took those principles, wrote the CLI to make them cheap, and then bolted on an eval harness so drift and regressions show up in benchmarks instead of vibes.

The video starts at the segment where the structured-planning argument lands. The full talk is worth watching if you've ever wondered why your agent is confidently producing the wrong code.

Originated by gsd-build Adapted for measurement Eval-first since v1.0

portfolio-sync

The public site at vendor/get-anything-done/site/ is a statically generated Next.js app that reads directly from the repo at build time. There is no CMS, no database, no API. The source of truth is the filesystem:

What you changed Where it lives How the site picks it up
New eval run (TRACE.json) evals/<project>/<version>/TRACE.json build-site-data.mjs → lib/eval-data.generated.ts
New finding evals/FINDINGS-YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md build-site-data.mjs → FINDINGS in catalog.generated.ts + /findings/<slug> route
New skill skills/<name>/SKILL.md Scanned into SKILLS catalog + /skills/<name> detail page
New subagent agents/<name>.md Scanned into AGENTS catalog + /agents/<name> detail page
New slash command commands/gad/<name>.md Scanned into COMMANDS catalog + /commands/<name> detail page
Eval template inherits a skill evals/<project>/template/skills/<skill>.md SKILL_INHERITANCE map → shown on /skills/<name> and catalog cards
New phase or task .planning/ROADMAP.xml, .planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml PLANNING_STATE → /planning meta-transparency page
New decision .planning/DECISIONS.xml< ... [truncated 16945 bytes] ... It's a static archive; everything that needs auth lives elsewhere. - **Don't build the games on Vercel.** 6+ `npm install` + `vite build` calls would balloon the build time. They're pre-built locally and committed. - **Don't hide the experiment's failure modes.** If a run scored 0 from a human reviewer, the site shows that honestly, side-by-side with the process metrics. The divergence is the point — it's what led to gad-29. - **Don't create MDX files in the site/content directory.** This site doesn't use MDX content at all (that was the old apps/portfolio/ plan). Content comes from scanning the repo. ## How this skill interacts with others - `create-skill` — when you write a new skill in `skills//SKILL.md`, it automatically shows up on the site after the next prebuild + deploy. No separate listing step. - `find-sprites` — unrelated to site sync, but both are "bootstrap" skills that get inherited into eval templates. If you change which skills are inherited where, the `SKILL_INHERITANCE` map on the site updates to reflect it. - Any eval skill (`eval-run`, `eval-preserve`, `eval-report`) — finishing a preserve triggers the sync. The run appears on `/runs//` on the next deploy. - `plan-phase` / `execute-phase` / `verify-work` — if this work lives in a GAD phase (and it should, per gad-18), the `/planning` page surfaces it automatically. Don't maintain parallel docs. ## Expected cadence The site should update at the same cadence as experimental progress. If you've done real work and the site looks the same, something's stale. If you're making commits without regenerating, you're drifting. Treat `npm run prebuild && npx next build` as the equivalent of `gad verify` for the public-facing side of the project.   <current-phase>22</current-phase>

  <next-action>Session 2026-04-10 SHIPPED all priority site fixes:

=== COMPLETED THIS SESSION ===

PRIORITY 1 — LANDING PAGE (all done):
(A) Rounds renamed to ExperimentLog with full filtering: round pagination, project dropdown, hypothesis chips, keyword search. Tags showing projects/workflows per round. TBD handling for unreviewed rounds.
(B) Results renamed to RoundResults — dynamic per round with dropdown selector. Per-round headlines from data/rounds.json. TBD section for unreviewed runs. Round conclusion callout from ROUND_SUMMARIES.
(C) HoverCard info panels on every run badge in Playable Archive — shows tokens, duration, hypothesis, condition, round, reqs version, composite, human score, gate status, review notes.
(D) LOOP SECTION pagination — deferred, not explicitly in components yet.

PRIORITY 2 — TECHNICAL STACK (all done):
(A) zustand 5.0.12 + immer 11.1.4 installed, filter-store.ts created with shared state replacing custom events.
(B) @tanstack/react-query 5.97.0 installed (available for future use).
(C) lucide-animated — not installed (requires shadcn registry, lucide-react already present).
(D) HoverCard component created as pure CSS/Tailwind (no Radix dependency, matches existing shadcn pattern).
(E) Rounds → ExperimentLog renamed everywhere. Results → RoundResults renamed.

PRIORITY 3 — DATA ARCHITECTURE (all done):
(A) data/ directory created with 5 JSON files: hypotheses.json, rounds.json, eval-conditions.json, graph-series.json, media.json.
(B) media.json holds YouTube video reference, imported by Lineage component.
(C) Broken screenshot references — deferred to starter repo update.

PRIORITY 4 — HOOK ERROR HANDLING (all done):
(A) .planning/ dir already created if missing (was already in code). Verified.
(B) Log rotation: MAX_TRACE_EVENTS=1000, truncates oldest events when exceeded.
(C) Hook error logging: logHookError() writes timestamped errors to ~/.claude/hooks.log with 100KB rotation. All catch blocks updated.

- User needs to play round 5 builds and score them
- Loop section pagination (Priority 1D) not yet done
- lucide-animated icons not installed

- No more evals until user has reviewed round 5

<state>
  <current-phase>22</current-phase>
  <current-plan></current-plan>
  <milestone>gad-v1.1</milestone>
  <status>active</status>
  <next-action>Session 2026-04-10 shipped site fixes + shadcn conversion + hypothesis diagrams.

=== COMPLETED ===

LANDING PAGE:
- Rounds → ExperimentLog: full filtering (round/project/hypothesis/keyword), tags per round, TBD handling
- Results → RoundResults: dynamic per round from data/rounds.json, TBD section, per-round conclusions
- HoverCard on Playable run badges: Radix-based shadcn HoverCard showing 10-field detail panel
- Hypothesis loop Mermaid diagrams: 3 tabbed diagrams (GAD loop, hypothesis tracks, experiment loop) in Workflow section
- WhatItIs section removed per user request

SHADCN FULL ADOPTION:
- Installed all shadcn components: HoverCard, Dialog, Popover, Select, Tabs, Tooltip, ScrollArea, DropdownMenu, Input, Badge, Button, Card, Separator
- tw-animate-css installed for shadcn animations
- Badge updated with success/danger variants matching existing site design
- Card updated with site's backdrop-blur aesthetic
- Playable modal → shadcn Dialog + ScrollArea
- RoundResults dropdown → shadcn Select
- Workflow diagrams → shadcn Tabs

ZUSTAND STORE:
- filter-store.ts: shared state for roundFilter, domainFilter, statusFilter, hypothesisFilter, searchQuery, selectedRunKey
- Playable.tsx migrated from custom events to zustand (backward compat with chart custom events maintained)
- ExperimentLog + RoundResults read from zustand store

DATA ARCHITECTURE:
- site/data/ directory: 5 static JSON files (hypotheses, rounds, eval-conditions, media, graph-series)
- Boundary: COMPUTED = lib/*.generated.ts (prebuild), STATIC = site/data/*.json + data/*.json (hand-curated)
- Build pipeline validates all site/data/*.json during prebuild
- Lineage uses media.json, RoundResults uses rounds.json

HOOK ERROR HANDLING:
- Log rotation: 1000 event max in .trace-events.jsonl
- logHookError(): timestamped errors to ~/.claude/hooks.log (100KB cap)
- All catch blocks use descriptive logging

DEPENDENCIES ADDED (site):
- zustand 5.0.12, immer 11.1.4, @tanstack/react-query 5.97.0
- @radix-ui/react-hover-card, @radix-ui/react-dialog, @radix-ui/react-popover, @radix-ui/react-select, @radix-ui/react-tabs, @radix-ui/react-tooltip, @radix-ui/react-scroll-area, @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu
- tw-animate-css

=== KNOWN ISSUES ===
- Hook errors (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1424) are non-blocking, from Claude Code's hook runner, not our hooks. Our hooks exit 0 cleanly when tested directly.
- lucide-animated icons not installed (requires shadcn registry add, not critical)
- Broken screenshot references in GitHub starter repo READMEs still present

=== NEXT SESSION ===
- User needs to play round 5 builds and score them
- Consider replacing remaining native <select> elements in Playable.tsx and ExperimentLog.tsx with shadcn Select
- Move CONVERSATION + diagram data from Workflow.tsx to data/*.json
- No more evals until user has reviewed round 5</next-action>

Display the complete GSD command reference. Output ONLY the reference content. Do NOT add project-specific analysis, git status, next-step suggestions, or any commentary beyond the reference.

<reference>
# GSD Command Reference

**GSD** (Get Shit Done) creates hierarchical project plans optimized for solo agentic development with Claude Code.

## Quick Start

1. `/gsd:new-project` - Initialize project (includes research, requirements, roadmap)
2. `/gsd:plan-phase 1` - Create detailed plan for first phase
3. `/gsd:execute-phase 1` - Execute the phase

## Staying Updated

GSD evolves fast. Update periodically:

```bash
npx get-anything-done@latest
```

## Core Workflow

```
/gsd:new-project → /gsd:plan-phase → /gsd:execute-phase → repeat
```

### Project Initialization

**`/gsd:new-project`**
Initialize new project through unified flow.

One command takes you from idea to ready-for-planning:
- Deep questioning to understand what you're building
- Optional domain research (spawns 4 parallel researcher agents)
- Requirements definition with v1/v2/out-of-scope scoping
- Roadmap creation with phase breakdown and success criteria

Creates all `.planning/` artifacts:
- `PROJECT.md` — vision and requirements
- `config.json` — workflow mode (interactive/yolo)
- `research/` — domain research (if selected)
- `REQUIREMENTS.md` — scoped requirements with REQ-IDs
- `ROADMAP.md` — phases mapped to requirements
- `STATE.md` — project memory

Usage: `/gsd:new-project`

**`/gsd:map-codebase`**
Map an existing codebase for brownfield projects.

- Analyzes codebase with parallel Explore agents
- Creates `.planning/codebase/` with 7 focused documents
- Covers stack, architecture, structure, conventions, testing, integrations, concerns
- Use before `/gsd:new-project` on existing codebases

Usage: `/gsd
... [truncated 16484 bytes] ...
/` to your `.gitignore`
- Useful for OSS contributions, client projects, or keeping planning private
- All planning files still work normally, just not tracked in git

**`planning.search_gitignored`** (default: `false`)
- `true`: Add `--no-ignore` to broad ripgrep searches
- Only needed when `.planning/` is gitignored and you want project-wide searches to include it

Example config:
```json
{
  "planning": {
    "commit_docs": false,
    "search_gitignored": true
  }
}
```

## Common Workflows

**Starting a new project:**

```
/gsd:new-project        # Unified flow: questioning → research → requirements → roadmap
/clear
/gsd:plan-phase 1       # Create plans for first phase
/clear
/gsd:execute-phase 1    # Execute all plans in phase
```

**Resuming work after a break:**

```
/gsd:progress  # See where you left off and continue
```

**Adding urgent mid-milestone work:**

```
/gsd:insert-phase 5 "Critical security fix"
/gsd:plan-phase 5.1
/gsd:execute-phase 5.1
```

**Completing a milestone:**

```
/gsd:complete-milestone 1.0.0
/clear
/gsd:new-milestone  # Start next milestone (questioning → research → requirements → roadmap)
```

**Capturing ideas during work:**

```
/gsd:add-todo                    # Capture from conversation context
/gsd:add-todo Fix modal z-index  # Capture with explicit description
/gsd:check-todos                 # Review and work on todos
/gsd:check-todos api             # Filter by area
```

**Debugging an issue:**

```
/gsd:debug "form submission fails silently"  # Start debug session
# ... investigation happens, context fills up ...
/clear
/gsd:debug                                    # Resume from where you left off
```

## Getting Help

- Read `.planning/PROJECT.md` for project vision
- Read `.planning/STATE.md` for current context
- Check `.planning/ROADMAP.md` for phase status
- Run `/gsd:progress` to check where you're up to
</reference>
